---
title: "LibriVox index JSON extractor"
---

Parses downloaded LibriVox book-page HTML files and writes one JSON file per input,
extracting the fields needed for speaker-level dataset construction.

**Output fields per page:**

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `title` | Book title from the page `<h1>` |
| `author` | Author string from `.book-page-author` |
| `online_text_link` | URL of the "Online text" anchor, if present |
| `chapters` | List of `{section, chapter, reader, reader_id, time}` from the chapter-download table |
| `cast_details` | List of `{character, reader_name, reader_id, reader_link}` from the cast description block (dramatic readings) |

**Usage:**

```bash
# Scan the default input directory
python3 extract-index-json.py

# Scan a custom directory, write JSON to a separate tree
python3 extract-index-json.py /data/index --outdir /data/json

# Process a single file
python3 extract-index-json.py /data/index/some-book/index.html
```

By default, output files are written beside the input HTML files, with the
`index` path component replaced by `json`. Use `--overwrite` to replace
existing JSON files.

**Dependency:** `beautifulsoup4`
